description: Execute the five stages of the data science pipeline — capture, curation, storage, analysis, and visualization — to transform raw big data into investment-ready insights

fields.name: pipeline_id
fields.label: Unique identifier of this pipeline run

fields.name: input_ref
fields.label: Upstream data references for this stage

data_capture.definition: Acquire raw data from source systems
data_capture.methods: API pulls, web scraping, sensor feeds, partner file deliveries

data_curation.definition: Clean, de-duplicate, reconcile, and normalise raw data

data_storage.options: Relational DB (Postgres), document DB (MongoDB), columnar store (Parquet), data warehouse (Redshift/BigQuery), data lake (S3)

data_analysis.definition: Apply statistical, econometric, or ML techniques to curated data
data_analysis.typical_steps: Feature engineering -> model selection -> training -> validation

data_visualization.definition: Present analytical output so stakeholders can interpret and act
data_visualization.forms: Time series charts, heat maps, tree maps, network graphs, dashboards
data_visualization.best_practices: Choose chart type matched to data shape; avoid misleading scales; annotate key events

structured_vs_unstructured_handling.structured: Ingest directly into relational or columnar store
structured_vs_unstructured_handling.semi_structured: Parse via JSON/XML schema, then flatten or keep nested
structured_vs_unstructured_handling.unstructured: Apply NLP, CV, or audio pipelines to extract features before storage

applications.esg_ingest: Capture regulatory filings -> curate ESG fields -> store in warehouse -> analyse -> dashboard
applications.alt_data_signals: Pull satellite imagery -> curate parking counts -> regression against sales
applications.client_reporting: Combine holdings + returns + benchmarks into visualised portfolio reports

validation.valid_stage: stage in {capture, curation, storage, analysis, visualization}

errors.message: Stage must be capture, curation, storage, analysis, or visualization

anti_patterns.rule: Do not skip data curation to speed up the pipeline
anti_patterns.why: Feeding dirty data to downstream models amplifies noise and hides bugs; spend the time upstream to save tenfold downstream

anti_patterns.rule: Do not store raw PII in data lakes without access controls
anti_patterns.why: Big-data storage is often less secure than structured DBs; expose PII only where absolutely needed and audit access

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on a single visualization chart type for all insights
anti_patterns.why: Line charts hide distributional features; heatmaps obscure time; choose form to match the question

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse data pipeline automation with reliability
anti_patterns.why: Automated pipelines silently propagate upstream errors; add data-quality checks and alerts at each stage

data_visualization_types: Tag cloud — frequency of keywords in text corpus
Mosaic plot — joint distribution of categoricals
Heat map — correlation or volatility surface
Network graph — firm or security interconnections
